findings.title: Collateral accounting credits nominal deposits instead of actual received balances

best_agent_match.title: Collateral accounting trusts nominal transfer amounts instead of actual received balances

findings.title: Interest accrual over-mints fees by charging on already-indexed debt

best_agent_match.title: Interest accrual over-mints fees by charging on already-indexed debt

findings.title: Fee minting can recurse into the manager's self-market debt token and brick interest updates

best_agent_match.title: Fee minting can recurse into the manager’s own debt token and brick all interest updates

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Reentrancy Guard in managePosition
rejected_candidates.reason: No concrete exploit path was shown. Reentrant `managePosition` calls still require the caller to be the position owner or a whitelisted delegate, and generic token-callback concerns here depend on owner-listed malicious collateral rather than an intrinsic protocol flaw.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Access Control on InterestRateDebtToken Index Updates
rejected_candidates.reason: `updateIndexAndPayFees()` being public is expected behavior for deterministic interest accrual. Calling it early only realizes accrued state; it does not grant privileged access or create a distinct exploit by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Price Feed Oracle Manipulation Risk
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic external-dependency concern, not a code-specific vulnerability demonstrated in this codebase.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Set Malicious Price Feed
rejected_candidates.reason: This is governance/admin trust, not an unintended permissionless vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Dangerous ERC20 Permit Signature Handling
rejected_candidates.reason: The code only applies a permit when `permitSignature.token` matches `r` or the selected `collateralToken`. A signature for any other token is ignored, so arbitrary-token permit misuse is not supported by the implementation.

rejected_candidates.title: Centralization Risk - Owner Can Disable All Collateral
rejected_candidates.reason: `setCollateralEnabled` only blocks new debt increases; existing users can still repay and withdraw because the enabled check is gated by `isDebtIncrease && debtChange > 0`. The claimed protocol-wide lockup is unsupported.

rejected_candidates.title: Flash Loan Callback Return Value Not Properly Validated
rejected_candidates.reason: The flash-loan code checks for the exact ERC3156 return value, and `RToken.flashLoan` is additionally protected by `nonReentrant`. The stated bypass is not supported by the code.

rejected_candidates.title: No Validation on indexIncreasePerSecond
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an owner-controlled economics parameter. Without a non-admin exploit path, it is a governance/configuration choice rather than a reportable vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Fee Recipient Change Without Timelock
rejected_candidates.reason: Changing the fee recipient is an explicit owner privilege, not an unintended vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Delegate Whitelisting Allows Arbitrary Delegates
rejected_candidates.reason: Users intentionally choosing whom to delegate to is expected functionality, not a protocol bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Position Closure With Zero Collateral Still Creates Debt Record
rejected_candidates.reason: The code clears `collateralTokenForPosition[position]` in `_closePosition` when both debt and collateral are zero, so the claimed stale debt record is not present.
